codeReview.summary: The fix fully closes the CLI help assertion gap (VAL-TUI-003), but the Ctrl+C graceful-exit proof remains incomplete because the new assertion can still pass from manual unmount cleanup rather than from Ctrl+C-triggered exit behavior.

issues.description: The test still calls `unmount()` before asserting `\x1b[?1049l`, so the restore-sequence evidence can come from manual unmount cleanup even if Ctrl+C exit handling regresses. This does not prove VAL-TUI-004's requirement that Ctrl+C itself triggers graceful exit/terminal restoration.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `tui-worker` skill says to write tests first (red), but this fix transcript shows implementation refactor before test edits while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is still marked true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session f0972efe-59a2-49a9-8d49-c69fde1f3f1a: sequence is Create src/cli.ts -> Edit src/index.tsx -> then Edit src/__tests__/cli.test.ts and src/__tests__/exit.test.tsx.

summary: Fail: CLI help assertions were fixed, but the Ctrl+C graceful-exit assertion remains non-causal because terminal-restore verification still happens after explicit manual unmount.
